  • runtime_engine holds 199 threads (65% of core total) — all perfectly stable with zero variance over 24h.
  • audio_engine runs 12 threads at 32% CPU — high per-thread workload indicating intensive audio processing.
  • video_engine (PID 9754): allocated, running, but consuming no CPU — investigate whether this is expected standby behavior.

6. MEMORY LEAK DETECTION

ProcessMemory Trend PatternRange (min to max)Monotonic Growth?Leak Risk

  • No classic memory leak signature (sustained monotonic growth) detected in any process over the 24h window.
  • node memory oscillation (~101 MB range) reflects dynamic allocation patterns — not accumulation. No leak indicated.
  • runtime_engine 0.3 MB drift in the final 20 minutes is at noise level — extend observation to 48h to assess.
  • Recommend re-evaluating node memory trend over next 48h given the upward movement seen at window end.

  • Loki returned zero log entries for service_name=stacktrace on this core instance in the full 24h window.
  • Absence of crash logs is fully consistent with Prometheus uptime data — all processes show monotonically increasing uptime with zero resets.
  • The core has been crash-free for the entire observation period.

8. METRICS AND LOG CORRELATION

Observed EventPrometheus SignalLoki SignalCorrelation Result

runtime_engine memory late uptick447.5 MB peak at 21:35 UTCNo logMinor drift, no log correlation

No crash eventsUptime monotonically rising across all PIDsZero stacktrace log entriesFull agreement — zero crashes confirmed by both signals
Process restartsZero uptime resets in 24h windowNo related logStable — no restart events to correlate
  • Prometheus and Loki signals are in full agreement: the core has been operationally stable with no crash or restart events.
  • audio_engine CPU elevation has no associated crash log — the process has been under sustained load without failure.
  • All WARNING-level observations are metric-only findings with no log-level corroboration, reducing their immediate severity.

9. UPTIME AND RESTART ANALYSIS

ProcessPIDUptime at Window StartUptime at Window EndCohortRestarts in 24hStatus

video_engine9755~11.62 days (1,003,800 s)~12.64 days (1,092,096 s)A — older0STABLE
engine_discover10475~11.62 days (1,003,800 s)~12.64 days (1,092,096 s)A — older0STABLE
video_engine9754~11.62 days (1,003,800 s)~12.64 days (1,092,096 s)A — older0STABLE
  • Cohort A (node, both video_engines, engine_discover): all started together ~11.6 days ago — likely initial system boot or full restart event.
  • Cohort B (audio_engine, runtime_engine): started approximately 8 days after Cohort A — ~4.1 days old at window start. This gap implies a targeted process restart approximately 3.5 days before the observation window. Root cause unknown.
  • Zero uptime resets observed during the 24h window — all six processes ran continuously throughout.

10. ANOMALIES

#AnomalyProcessObservationSeverity
1Sustained High CPU Utilizationaudio_engine (PID 16128)32.02% avg user CPU over 24h; peak 34.72% at 08:45 UTC. Approximately 60x higher than runtime_engine. CPU range of 2.76% indicates sustained load, not a transient spike.WARNING
2Node Memory Upward Trendnode (PID 8933)Memory utilization rose from 6.25% at window start to 6.88% peak and ended at 6.82%. Total upward movement of ~0.57% over 24h. Non-monotonic but trending upward in the final hours.WARNING
3video_engine Idle / Suspendedvideo_engine (PID 9754)Zero CPU usage, only 1 thread, 7.24 MB memory for the entire 24h period. Process appears allocated but completely disengaged from workload.WARNING
4Runtime Engine Late-Window Memory Driftruntime_engine (PID 16730)Memory reached 447.5 MB peak at 21:35 UTC — a 0.3 MB drift from the 24h average, observed only in the final 20 minutes of the window.WATCH
5Historical Cohort B Restartaudio_engine, runtime_engineThese two processes share an uptime ~8 days shorter than Cohort A processes, indicating they were restarted together approximately 3.5 days before this observation window. Cause not recorded in current log window.INFORMATIONAL

11. SUMMARY AND RISK ASSESSMENT

Overall Core Status: WARNING

DomainStatusKey Finding
CPUWARNINGaudio_engine sustained at 32.02% avg user CPU — elevated workload dominating core CPU budget
MemoryWARNINGnode memory trending upward (+0.57% over 24h); not leaking but growing — monitor closely
Crash EventsHEALTHYZero crash events; zero Loki stacktrace log entries in 24h window
RestartsHEALTHYZero process restarts in the 24h observation window
Thread HealthHEALTHYAll thread counts stable with zero variance — no thread leak or explosion indicators
I/O PerformanceHEALTHYZero I/O wait across all processes — no disk or network blocking
Process AvailabilityWARNINGvideo_engine (PID 9754) idle and unengaged for entire 24h — confirm operational intent
Memory Leak RiskHEALTHYNo monotonic growth pattern detected in any process — no active memory leak

The core is operational and crash-free. No process has failed or restarted during the observed period. The primary risk indicators are audio_engine sustained CPU load and node memory gradual growth. Both are pre-failure signals that require investigation to prevent escalation. The overall classification is WARNING — the core requires attention but is not in a critical state.

12. RECOMMENDED ACTIONS

PriorityActionTargetRationale
HIGHProfile audio_engine for CPU hotspotsaudio_engine (PID 16128)32.02% avg CPU is significantly elevated. Use process profiling to identify the specific function or workload driving sustained consumption. Check for inefficient codec processing or audio routing loops.
MEDIUMSet a memory alert threshold for nodenode (PID 8933)Memory is trending from 6.25% to 6.88% over 24h. Configure an alert at 8% utilization to catch continued growth before it becomes a memory pressure event.
MEDIUMInvestigate video_engine (PID 9754) idle statevideo_engine (PID 9754)Process is running with 1 thread and zero CPU for 24h. Confirm this is intentional standby behavior. If not expected, the process may be hung or mis-configured.
MEDIUMMonitor runtime_engine memory over next 48hruntime_engine (PID 16730)0.3 MB drift in the final 20 minutes of the window may be the start of a growth trend. Track over the next 48h to determine if this is noise or accumulation.
LOWInvestigate root cause of Cohort B restartaudio_engine, runtime_engineThese processes restarted ~3.5 days before this window. Check older logs or incident records to determine if the restart was planned or caused by a failure.
LOWValidate metric scrape configurationAll processesOne missing data point per series (143/144). Verify that the scrape interval is correctly aligned with the retention policy to ensure complete coverage.
